summary: Joel announces a small new feature for the Joel on Software discussion groups: registered user accounts with visual indicators and article favoriting. The feature is built on an early version of FogBugz 6, which they are dogfooding internally before its beta release. The post uses a playful FAQ format to address basic questions about the new accounts. It ends with a teasing reference to a mysterious 'kiwi' mascot.
key_points: Registered accounts are optional and free, with a green checkbox displayed next to logged-in usernames
The discussion group software is an early internal build of FogBugz 6, being used as dogfooding before public beta
Joel links back to his own essay on the importance of eating your own dogfood as a development practice
money_quotes: The software running the discussion groups is our in-house, under-development, unannounced version of FogBugz 6, which will be going to beta later this summer, and we're rolling it out now as a part of our continuing efforts to eat our own dogfood.
key_insight: Fog Creek practiced dogfooding by running their community discussion forums on a pre-release version of FogBugz 6 to test it in production before public beta.
